config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can quickly become an excuse for abuse by those in power. A healthy society protects basic rights first, then balances them with the common good when there is a real and necessary reason. From my perspective, the strongest communities are built when people are treated as individuals with dignity, not just as tools for collective goals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, prinsip hak individu itu penting justru karena sejarah kita penuh contoh ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, diskriminasi, dan pembatasan kebebasan. Kepentingan masyarakat memang nyata, tetapi menurut saya ia harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan seenaknya. Jika hak individu bisa disisihkan setiap kali mayoritas menganggapnya mengganggu, maka yang terlindungi bukan masyarakat, melainkan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should generally come before the individual, because in practice that often becomes a shortcut for elites or the state to silence people. In Indonesia, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used to justify weak accountability, so I am cautious about any argument that gives society blanket priority over rights. Society matters, but it is strongest when basic rights are protected first and any limits are narrow, necessary, and truly accountable. If individual rights are treated as disposable, then the society being defended is already on the wrong path.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, dan sejarah kami memberi banyak alasan untuk waspada terhadap itu. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih sehat kalau dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak bisa dikorbankan hanya karena mayoritas atau negara merasa terganggu. Kalau hak individu bisa disisihkan kapan saja atas nama masyarakat, maka yang menang biasanya bukan masyarakat, melainkan pihak yang paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take blanket priority over individual rights. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, that idea is too easy to abuse, because “for the public good” is often used to excuse weak accountability, pressure from authorities, or silencing people who are inconvenient. Society does matter, but it should be pursued through narrow, necessary limits that still respect basic rights, not by treating individuals as disposable. If a community needs to crush individual dignity to function, then it is not a healthy society in the first place.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras pernyataan itu kalau dipakai sebagai aturan umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk menekan hak sipil, kebebasan berpendapat, dan perlakuan setara. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar agar mayoritas atau negara tidak semena-mena atas nama kebaikan bersama. Memang ada situasi darurat di mana pembatasan terbatas bisa dibenarkan, tetapi harus sempit, perlu, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan alasan default. Kalau hak pribadi bisa dikorbankan kapan saja, maka yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat sering kali hanya kepentingan pihak yang paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
